project_lifecycle.title: Project Lifecycle
project_lifecycle.description: Stages of a software project from inception to deployment and maintenance.
project_lifecycle.insight: Every language has a different philosophy: C++ is modular; Go is simplicity; Python is flexibility; TypeScript mirrors Node conventions; Scala bridges JVM tooling.

collection_mappings.title: Collection Mappings
collection_mappings.description: Mapping and transforming collections in different programming languages.
collection_mappings.insight: Use this table to quickly find the right data structure in your target language. Performance characteristics vary: dynamic arrays are fast for append; linked lists are fast for insertion; hash maps prioritize lookup speed.

nullable_optional_values.title: Nullable & Optional Values
nullable_optional_values.description: Working with nullable and optional values in different programming languages.

insight.text: Understanding how different languages handle the absence of values is crucial for writing robust code. C++ uses pointers and std::optional; Python uses None; TypeScript has null and undefined; Go has nil; Scala has Option. Each approach has implications for error handling and API design.

type_system_comparison.title: Type System Comparison
type_system_comparison.description: Comparing type systems in different programming languages.
type_system_comparison.insight: Use this as a context switch cheat: nominal vs structural typing, and static vs dynamic guarantees, changes how you design APIs and tests.

scala_migration.title: Scala Migration
scala_migration.description: Guidelines and best practices for migrating code to Scala.
scala_migration.insight: Treat Scala 3 as an ergonomic evolution: same core FP/OOP model, but cleaner syntax and clearer contextual abstractions.
